message:
feat(sdk): implement programmatic session embedding API with create_agent_session()

Add the full programmatic entrypoint for non-CLI consumers that want to
run Pi agent sessions in-process. This completes the SDK surface that was
previously limited to type re-exports.

**SessionOptions**: Configuration struct mirroring the core CLI startup
parameters — provider, model, API key, thinking level, system prompt,
tool selection, working directory, session persistence, extension paths,
and extension/repair policies. Defaults to ephemeral (no_session: true)
with 50 max tool iterations.

**AgentSessionHandle**: Lightweight wrapper around AgentSession exposing
a high-level `prompt()` method for send-one-message-get-one-response
usage, with full access to the underlying AgentSession via session(),
session_mut(), and into_inner() for advanced consumers.

**create_agent_session()**: Orchestrator that mirrors the CLI startup
path in src/main.rs — loads config, refreshes OAuth tokens, initializes
model registry, creates session, resolves model selection and thinking
level, builds system prompt, creates provider, constructs tool registry,
sets up compaction settings, optionally loads extensions with policy, and
restores message history from persisted sessions. Handles all the
non-obvious setup that would otherwise require deep knowledge of Pi
internals (auth refresh, model scoping, thinking budget resolution,
session arc wrapping, extension policy resolution).

**Tests** (3 integration tests):
- create_agent_session_default_succeeds: Verifies default options produce
  a working session with anthropic/claude-sonnet-4 provider
- create_agent_session_respects_provider_model_and_thinking: Verifies
  provider/model/thinking overrides propagate correctly
- create_agent_session_no_session_keeps_ephemeral_state: Verifies
  no_session mode produces unsaved, pathless sessions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
